items.title:
You were suspended for platform manipulation.

On April 11, we announced that a portion of creator revenue would be allo
items.content:
You were suspended for platform manipulation.

On April 11, we announced that a portion of creator revenue would be allocated to original authors of content. Immediately after, you stopped using Video Share, which you had been using for 3 years. 

Instead, you began to programmatically download-and re-upload other accounts' videos so that the system would credit them as original.

The behavior alone was circumstantial. What made it conclusive: you uploaded another user's video with the  watermark cropped out. You deliberately attempted to manipulate the payout formula.

We don't pay people who cheat the program.

items.title:
Those are orthogonal concepts. 
- World models trained on highly diverse data become foundation models: their encoders c
items.content:
Those are orthogonal concepts. 
- World models trained on highly diverse data become foundation models: their encoders can be used for a wide variety of downstream tasks. 
- "World"  refers to two things: (1) predicting the evolution of a complex system or environment, (2) predicting the evolution of a system under control and its effect on the environment (action-conditioned world model) which is a necessary component of planning.

items.title:
The goal of training a machine learning model is loss minimization. 

That was just being applied to algorithms, and now
items.content:
The goal of training a machine learning model is loss minimization. 

That was just being applied to algorithms, and now it's being applied to whole enterprises. 

Loss minimization is one thing that the self-learning loop is affording leaders right now.

You're no longer losing insights from your customers because AI is embedded in your ticketing system. You're no longer losing insights from your team chats because AI is in Slack. You're no longer losing decisions from your leadership because AI is inside of your meetings. 

We've been leaving data value on the table for decades. 

AI as an operating system is really aiming to capture that value and compound it.

items.title: I don’t know who needs to hear this, but you don’t need to know how to use every ai tool or every new feature shipped ev
items.content:
I don’t know who needs to hear this, but you don’t need to know how to use every ai tool or every new feature shipped every week.

ai is moving too fast, even faster than its creators. and every time a new feature is released, the previous one still remains useful.

what you should focus on instead is becoming fluent with the tools that actually bring you value, especially monetary value.

you don’t need to learn every AI tool in the market. focus on the simple basics:

• understand how LLMs think
• understand why agents hallucinate
• understand context windows
• understand how to use your tokens properly

If you understand these basics, you’ll become confident enough that whenever a new feature drops, 

you’ll already know how to work around it and use it effectively.

items.title: Show HN: Open-Source AI Racing Harness
items.content:
Show HN: Open-Source AI Racing Harness Hi I'm Dan from Elodin, making an open source real-time capable flight software simulation.

For AI Grand Prix contestants, the wait for the Round 1 virtual qualifier simulation has been grueling.

If you’re competing, check out our simulation harness to tide you over, built to match the published competition constraints and message format. It runs against real Betaflight, which we learned requires at least 1000 sensor samples per second to run real-time correctly.

The competition warranted introducing a new feature to generate the camera sensor directly in the simulation loop. Typically people connect to Unreal or similar game engine to create a camera sensor, which works well but is very heavy. For the simple needs of this challenge, creating sample directly in the loop is very handy and easy to use. Happy to hear your feedback on this! While it's not fancy looking currently, it uses the Rust Bevy game engine, which should allow us to improve the visual fidelity quickly.

We all should easily be able to shift our implementation to the published competition sim once it lands. Hope you enjoy and good luck!

ALL YOU NEED IS THESE 5 PROMPTS TO RUN A MULTI-AGENT CLAUDE SYSTEM:

1. “Act as an autonomous software engineering team.
items.content:
ALL YOU NEED IS THESE 5 PROMPTS TO RUN A MULTI-AGENT CLAUDE SYSTEM:

1. “Act as an autonomous software engineering team. Plan, delegate, code, test, and iterate across multiple agents until the feature is production-ready.”

2. “Spin up specialized agents for research, implementation, QA, and documentation. Agents should coordinate independently and only report major decisions.”

3. “Create a self-managing workflow of AI agents that decomposes this project into tasks, assigns ownership, reviews outputs, and continuously improves results.”

4. “Operate like an AI startup team: one agent manages strategy, others execute coding, debugging, testing, and deployment with minimal user intervention.”

5. “Run a multi-agent system where agents can create subtasks, critique each other’s work, and recursively refine outputs until all requirements are satisfied.”

It doesn’t have to be complicated.
